policy_title: Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Computed Tomography (CT) of the Spine — Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin 0236

service.description: MRI lumbar spine without IV contrast. CPB 0236 uses an "any of the following" medical-necessity list: two bullets are time-thresholded (radiculopathy 6 weeks, spondylolisthesis/DDD 4 weeks); the remaining bullets function as red-flag bypasses.

red_flags.label: Clinical suspicion of spinal cord or cauda equina compression syndrome
red_flags.description: Clinical suspicion of spinal cord or cauda equina compression syndrome.
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: cord/cauda equina compression)

red_flags.label: Clinical evidence of spinal stenosis
red_flags.description: Clinical evidence of spinal stenosis (e.g., neurogenic claudication, bilateral leg pain with walking relieved by sitting).

red_flags.label: Congenital anomalies or deformities of the spine
red_flags.description: Congenital anomalies or deformities of the spine.

red_flags.label: Lumbar epidural lipomatosis (diagnosis and evaluation)
red_flags.description: Lumbar epidural lipomatosis — diagnosis and evaluation.

red_flags.label: Recurrent symptoms after spinal surgery
red_flags.description: Recurrent symptoms following previous spinal surgery.
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: recurrent post-surgical symptoms)

red_flags.label: Evaluation prior to epidural injection
red_flags.description: Evaluation prior to an epidural injection to rule out tumor/infection and localize the injection.

red_flags.label: Follow-up evaluation for spinal malignancy or spinal infection
red_flags.description: Follow-up evaluation for a known spinal malignancy or spinal infection.

red_flags.label: Known or suspected myelopathy (including MS)
red_flags.description: Known or suspected myelopathy, including multiple sclerosis (e.g., hyperreflexia, Hoffmann sign, spastic gait).

red_flags.label: Known or suspected primary spinal cord tumor
red_flags.description: Known or suspected primary spinal cord tumor.
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: primary cord tumor)

red_flags.label: Primary spinal bone tumors or suspected vertebral/paraspinal/intraspinal metastases
red_flags.description: Primary spinal bone tumors, or suspected vertebral / paraspinal / intraspinal metastases (e.g., history of breast/lung/prostate cancer with new back pain, lytic lesion on x-ray, night pain).
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: spinal bone tumor/metastases)

red_flags.label: Progressively severe symptoms despite conservative management
red_flags.description: Progressively severe symptoms despite documented conservative management. Duration/severity are not defined numerically in the policy — clinician judgment applies.

red_flags.label: Rapidly progressing neurological deficit, or major motor weakness
red_flags.description: Rapidly progressing neurological deficit, or major motor weakness (e.g., foot drop, inability to walk).
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: rapid neurological deficit)

red_flags.label: Severe back pain (e.g., requiring hospitalization)
red_flags.description: Severe back pain requiring hospitalization or IV opioids.
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: severe back pain)

red_flags.label: Suspected infectious process (osteomyelitis, epidural abscess, soft tissue)
red_flags.description: Suspected infectious process: osteomyelitis, epidural abscess, or soft-tissue spinal infection (e.g., fever, IV drug use, elevated ESR/CRP, positive blood cultures).

red_flags.label: Suspected spinal cord injury secondary to trauma
red_flags.description: Suspected spinal cord injury secondary to trauma.
red_flags.source_section: CPB 0236 medical-necessity list (bullet: SCI from trauma)

red_flags.label: Suspected spinal fracture/dislocation secondary to trauma (plain films not conclusive)
red_flags.description: Suspected spinal fracture or dislocation secondary to trauma where plain films are not conclusive.

red_flags.label: Suspected transverse myelitis
red_flags.description: Suspected transverse myelitis (e.g., sensory level, acute myelopathy).

pathways.description: Persistent back or neck pain with radiculopathy as evidenced by pain plus objective findings of motor or reflex changes in the specific nerve root distribution, with no improvement after 6 weeks of conservative therapy.
Spondylolisthesis or degenerative disease of the spine that has not responded to 4 weeks of conservative therapy.

conservative_therapy.notes: Per CPB 0236 footnote verbatim: "Conservative therapy = moderate activity, analgesics, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, muscle relaxants." PHYSICAL_THERAPY is NOT named in CPB 0236 (it is addressed in related CPB 0016 for injections) and is deliberately omitted here — utilization-review practice may differ from footnote letter, flagged as clinician-review gap. EPIDURAL_INJECTION, SPINAL_MANIPULATION, GABAPENTINOID, ORAL_CORTICOSTEROID, HOME_EXERCISE, EDUCATION, OCCUPATIONAL_THERAPY are not named and are excluded from accepted_kinds. Footnote is ambiguous on whether all four named items must co-occur or whether a subset satisfies the trial; engine uses min_kinds_count=1 for v1.

prior_imaging.narrative: No numeric lookback window is published. Background cites evidence that repeat/updated imaging does not reduce revision rates in adults with lumbar degenerative disease (Ries 2018; Lee 2019) and discourages serial imaging absent clinical change. Repeat MRI in different positions (flexion/extension/rotation/lateral bending) and with/without weight-bearing is deemed experimental/investigational.

notes: ICD-10 exclusions: none apply to lumbar MRI under CPB 0236. The policy's only explicit ICD-10 exclusion is R40.20–R40.2444 (Coma) scoped to "routine MRI after a normal CT of the cervical spine" — that is cervical-specific and does NOT apply to CPT 72148. Screening / asymptomatic surveillance (Z-code space) is excluded implicitly via the medical-necessity-list requirement, not via an explicit exclusion list. Experimental / investigational items not encoded as rule inputs for v1 but relevant for reasoning trace: BoneMRI (MRI-based synthetic CT), dynamic-kinetic MRI of the cervical spine, dual-energy CT for bone-marrow edema in acute vertebral fractures. CPB 0236 does not specify an age cutoff. Related policies: CPB 0016 (Back Pain: Invasive Procedures) uses a 6-week threshold for SI-joint injections, consistent with the 6-week threshold here for radiculopathy; CPB 0743 (Spinal Surgery) refers back to imaging policies and does not restate MRI criteria.
